revision.comment: 追加
revision.text:
雲甘寺坐楢本神社(うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ)は、奈良県生駒郡平群町梨本にある式内社。
== 概要 == 三代実録巻第五に「貞観三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」とでている。
旧社地は、約500m東の雲甘寺の鎮守社だったが、寺が明治元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。
祭神は、菊理姫命
但し、江戸時代には白山権現、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。
本殿の右の境内社は、春日社で祭神は天児屋根命。

revision.comment: 追加
revision.text:
雲甘寺坐楢本神社(うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ)は、奈良県生駒郡平群町梨本にある式内社。
== 概要 == 三代実録巻第五に「貞観三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」とでている。
旧社地は、約500m東の雲甘寺の鎮守社だったが、寺が明治元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。
祭神は、菊理姫命
但し、江戸時代には白山権現、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。
本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は天児屋根命。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は稲荷大神。
社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、天照大神と、一言主神を祀る。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/47570984|publisher=Hegurichō|date=1976|oclc=47570984|last=平群町史編集委員会.|pages=645-647}}</ref>

但し、江戸時代には白山権現、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。
本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は天児屋根命。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は稲荷大神。
社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、天照大神と、一言主神を祀る。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/47570984|publisher=Hegurichō|date=1976|oclc=47570984|last=平群町史編集委員会.|pages=645-647}}</ref>

[[祭神]]は、[[菊理姫命]]
但し、[[江戸時代]]には[[白山権現]]、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。
本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は[[稲荷神]]。
社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、[[天照大神]]と、[[一言主|一言主神]]を祀る。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=平群町史|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/47570984|publisher=Hegurichō|date=1976|oclc=47570984|last=平群町史編集委員会.|pages=645-647}}</ref>

revision.comment: 画像追加
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | 画像 = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]] | 所在地 = 奈良県生駒郡平群町梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 祭神 = 菊理姫命 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本にある[[式内社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は、[[村社]]であり[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]。
== 概要 == [[日本三代実録|三代実録]]巻第五に「[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」とでている。
旧社地は、約500[[メートル]]東の雲甘寺の[[鎮守社]]だったが、寺が[[明治]]元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。
[[祭神]]は、[[菊理姫命]]。
ただし、[[江戸時代]]には([[白山信仰]]と思われる)[[白山権現]]、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。
本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は[[稲荷神]]。
社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、[[天照大神]]と、[[一言主|一言主神]]を祀る<ref>{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うんかんしにいますならもとしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{神社 | 名称 = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | 画像 = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 祭神 = [[菊理姫命]] | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は、[[村社]]であり[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]。 == 概要 == [[日本三代実録|三代実録]]巻第五に「[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500[[メートル]]東の雲甘寺の[[鎮守社]]だったが、寺が[[明治]]元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 [[祭神]]は、[[菊理姫命]]。 [[江戸時代]]には([[白山信仰]]と思われる)[[白山権現]]、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は[[稲荷神]]。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、[[天照大神]]と、[[一言主|一言主神]]を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に[[御神木]]が祀られている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うんかんしにいますならもとしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine | native_name = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | image = Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.633654|135.69826}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

revision.comment: 29 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | 画像 = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 祭神 = [[菊理姫命]] | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は、[[村社]]であり[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]。 == 概要 == [[日本三代実録|三代実録]]巻第五に「[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500[[メートル]]東の雲甘寺の[[鎮守社]]だったが、寺が[[明治]]元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 [[祭神]]は、[[菊理姫命]]。 [[江戸時代]]には([[白山信仰]]と思われる)[[白山権現]]、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は[[稲荷神]]。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、[[天照大神]]と、[[一言主|一言主神]]を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に[[御神木]]が祀られている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うんかんしにいますならもとしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = [[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[村社]] | 祭神 = [[菊理姫命]] | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[生駒郡]][[平群町]]梨本に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は、[[村社]]であり[[神饌幣帛料供進社]]。 == 概要 == [[日本三代実録|三代実録]]巻第五に「[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500[[メートル]]東の雲甘寺の[[鎮守社]]だったが、寺が[[明治]]元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 [[祭神]]は、[[菊理姫命]]。 [[江戸時代]]には([[白山信仰]]と思われる)[[白山権現]]、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は[[稲荷神]]。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、[[天照大神]]と、[[一言主|一言主神]]を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に[[御神木]]が祀られている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Unranked shrines (shinkai)]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[JA:雲甘寺坐楢本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q119929664}} [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|雲甘寺坐楢本神社|version=107399671|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称 = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | 画像 = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|菊理姫命}} | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社}}であり{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}。 == 概要 == {{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981|三代実録}}巻第五に「{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500{{jalink|メートル|Q11573}}東の雲甘寺の{{jalink|鎮守社|Q13479654}}だったが、寺が{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 {{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|菊理姫命}}。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には({{jalink|白山信仰|Q11579557}}と思われる){{jalink|白山権現|Q11579578}}、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665}}。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}と、{{jalink|一言主|Q10864079|一言主神}}を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に{{jalink|御神木}}が祀られている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}}

{{nihongo|'''Unkanjiniimasunaramoto Shrine'''|雲甘寺坐楢本神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県生駒郡平群町梨木', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:雲甘寺坐楢本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q119929664}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 雲甘寺坐楢本神社 | 画像 = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|菊理姫命}} | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}であり{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}。 === 概要 === {{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981|三代実録}}巻第五に「{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500{{jalink|メートル|Q11573}}東の雲甘寺の{{jalink|鎮守社|Q13479654}}だったが、寺が{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 {{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|菊理姫命}}。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には({{jalink|白山信仰|Q11579557}}と思われる){{jalink|白山権現|Q11579578}}、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665}}。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}と、{{jalink|一言主|Q10864079|一言主神}}を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に{{jalink|御神木}}が祀られている。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うんかんしにいますならもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本298 | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 37 | 緯度秒 = 56 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 41 | 経度秒 = 54 | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|菊理姫命}} | 地図 = Japan Nara |別名=野間田明神 }} '''雲甘寺坐楢本神社'''（うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}{{jalink|平群町|Q1346790}}梨本に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}であり{{jalink|神饌幣帛料供進社}}。 === 概要 === {{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981|三代実録}}巻第五に「{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}三年十月二十二日 (略) 雲感寺無位猶本神社従五位下」と記されている。 旧社地は、約500{{jalink|メートル|Q11573}}東の雲甘寺の{{jalink|鎮守社|Q13479654}}だったが、寺が{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年に廃されたので、現在地に移された。 {{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}は、{{jalink|菊理姫命}}。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には({{jalink|白山信仰|Q11579557}}と思われる){{jalink|白山権現|Q11579578}}、明治時代には野間田明神と呼ばれた。 本殿の向かって左側の境内社は、春日社で祭神は{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508|天児屋根命}}。そのすぐ隣の境内社は、稲荷社で祭神は{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665}}。 社殿の左塀の外の神饌所には、{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}と、{{jalink|一言主|Q10864079|一言主神}}を祀る{{Cite book|和書|editor=平群町史編集委員会|title=平群町史|publisher=平群町役場|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}。 境内に{{jalink|御神木}}が祀られている。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うんかんしにいますならもとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} located in Nashimoto, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}}. Under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking}}, it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} and is a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{jalink|guardian shrine|Q13479654}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}}. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called {{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}} (thought to be associated with {{jalink|Hakusan faith|Q11579557}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Inari Ōkami|Q719665}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{jalink|sacred tree}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unkanjiniimasunaramotojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} located in Nashimoto, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}}. Under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking}}, it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} and is a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{jalink|guardian shrine|Q13479654}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}}. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called {{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}} (thought to be associated with {{jalink|Hakusan faith|Q11579557}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Inari Ōkami|Q719665}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{jalink|sacred tree}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unkanjiniimasunaramotojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{jalink|sacred tree}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unkanjiniimasunaramotojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} | Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} located in Nashimoto, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}{{jalink|Heguri Town|Q1346790}}. Under the {{jalink|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking}}, it was classified as a {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} and is a {{jalink|shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{jalink|guardian shrine|Q13479654}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{jalink|meters|Q11573}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} is {{jalink|Kikurihime-no-Mikoto}}. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called {{jalink|Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578}} (thought to be associated with {{jalink|Hakusan faith|Q11579557}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{jalink|Inari Ōkami|Q719665}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}} and {{jalink|Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{jalink|sacred tree}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:unkanjiniimasunaramotojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:平群町の建築物]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|WD=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth '奈良県生駒郡平群町梨木', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:雲甘寺坐楢本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q119929664}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine | Image = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:平群町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

'奈良県生駒郡平群町梨木', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:雲甘寺坐楢本神社]] {{wikidata link|Q119929664}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine | Image = [[File:Naramoto Jinja (Heguri).JPG|250px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Nashimoto 298 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 37 | Latitude seconds = 56 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 54 | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = Japan Nara | Alternate name = Nomata Myōjin }} '''Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine''' (うんかんじにいますならもとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} located in Nashimoto, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|WD=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Overview === In volume five of the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, it is recorded: “On the twenty-second day of the tenth month of the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (abridged), Unkanji, unranked Naramoto Shrine, was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The former shrine site was the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q13479654|WD=Q13479654|1=Chinjusha}} of Unkanji Temple, about 500 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|WD=Q11573|1=Metre}} to the east, but when the temple was abolished in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was relocated to its present location. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called {{ill|lt=Hakusan Gongen|Q11579578|WD=Q11579578|1=Hakusan Gongen}} (thought to be associated with {{ill|lt=Hakusan faith|Q11579557|WD=Q11579557|1=Hakusan Faith}}), and in the Meiji period it was known as Nomata Myōjin. The subsidiary shrine on the left side facing the main hall is the Kasuga Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. Immediately beside it is the Inari Shrine, whose enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Inari Ōkami|Q719665|WD=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}. At the offering hall outside the left fence of the shrine buildings, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi-no-Kami|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi|1=Hitokotonushi}} are enshrined{{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=645-647}}. A {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sacred tree|1=UNKNOWN}} is enshrined within the precincts. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Unkanji-niimasu Naramoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
